title: Float valve comprising a holding means
abstract: A float valve comprises a valve body comprising an inlet and an outlet. The inlet and the outlet are arranged to be able to be in fluid communication with each other. The float valve comprises a valve arrangement having an open configuration in which fluid can flow between the inlet and the outlet, and a closed configuration in which fluid flow between the inlet and the outlet can be prevented- The float valve comprises an arm pivotably mounted to the valve body and moveable with respect to the valve body. The arm is able to cooperate with the valve arrangement so as to be able to move the valve arrangement between the open and closed configurations. The valve comprises a float coupled to the arm, and a holding means arranged to be able to hold the valve arrangement in the open configuration until acted upon by an arm force from the arm greater than a threshold holding force of the holding arrangement so that the valve arrangement can be caused to move towards the closed configuration when the arm force is greater than the threshold holding force.
claims: Claims Claims 1 . A float valve comprising: a valve body comprising an inlet and an outlet, the inlet and the outlet being arranged to be able to be in fluid communication with each other; a valve arrangement having an open configuration in which fluid can flow between the inlet and the outlet, and a closed configuration in which fluid flow between the inlet and the outlet can be prevented; an arm pivotably mounted to the valve body and moveable with respect to the valve body, the arm being able to cooperate with the valve arrangement so as to be able to move the valve arrangement between the open and closed configurations; a float coupled to the arm; and holding means arranged to be able to hold the valve arrangement in the open configuration until acted upon by an arm force from the arm greater than a threshold holding force of the holding arrangement so that the valve arrangement can be caused to move towards the closed configuration when the arm force is greater than the threshold holding force. 2. A float valve according to any preceding claim, in which the holding means is arranged to be able to hold the arm to the valve body. 3. A float valve according to claim 1 or claim 2, in which the holding means comprises a first magnet. 4. A float valve according to claim 3, comprising a valve operating element coupled to the arm and arranged to act between the arm and the valve arrangement such that the valve arrangement can be moved between the open and closed configurations in response to movement by the arm. 5. A float valve according to claim 4, in which the first magnet is arranged to be able to exert a force between the valve operating element and the valve body so as to be able to hold the valve arrangement in the open position. 6. A float valve according to claim 4 or claim 5, comprising a resilient element coupled between the valve operating element and the arm, the resilient element being arranged so that force from the arm can be transmitted through the resilient element to the valve operating element. 7. A float valve according to claim 6, in which the resilient element is a helical spring. 8. A float valve according to any of claims 4 to 7, comprising an attracting means arranged to be able to attract the valve operating element to a position where the valve arrangement is in the closed configuration when an attractive force provided by the attracting means is greater than a force from the arm which urges the valve arrangement towards the open configuration. 9. A float valve according to any of claims 4 to 8, in which the valve operating element comprises the first magnet. 10. A float valve according to claim 8, in which the attracting means comprises a second magnet and the valve operating element comprises a magnetic material arranged to be able to cooperate with the first magnet and the second magnet. . 1 1 . A float valve according to any of claims 3 to 10, in which the first magnet is held by a snap fit. 12. A float valve according to any preceding claim, in which the arm is integrally formed with the float. 13. A valve according to any preceding claim, in which the valve is configured to be able to cooperate with a smart meter so as to monitor flow and use of liquid by a consumer.
